response.justification: The candidate is currently working on AI-agent infrastructure and MCP frameworks at re:cinq—directly aligned with the role's focus on agentic workflows. However, the resume demonstrates only ~1 year of explicitly AI-focused work and doesn't substantiate the required 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions. Critical gaps include no Python (required language), no demonstrated experience with data pipelines, vector databases, or RAG architectures, and no evidence of LLM performance optimization work. The current role is promising and suggests potential depth, but the resume lacks sufficient proof of the required AI/ML infrastructure expertise. Worth screening to validate these specific capabilities.
strengths: Currently working on MCP code generators and agentic workflows—directly relevant to job's core focus on production-grade AI system delivery
Demonstrated staff-level technical leadership across multiple companies with proven ability to mentor engineers and organize technical training
Strong TypeScript expertise and solid cloud platform experience (AWS CDK, Kubernetes, Docker) with deep DevOps and infrastructure background
concerns: Python is required but absent from resume despite needing 8+ years of AI development in Python or TypeScript
Resume shows only ~1 year of explicitly AI-focused work; doesn't demonstrate the minimum 8+ years building production-grade AI solutions
No evidence of critical AI/ML infrastructure experience: data pipelines for structured/unstructured data, vector databases, RAG architectures, or LLM optimization

key_factors.factor: Current Role in Agentic AI Infrastructure
Missing Python Language and ML Infrastructure Skills
